title: Syncope follow-up

investigations.text: Supports clinician-led orthostatic assessment, repeat or previous ECG review, ambulatory ECG selection, further investigation, and specialist cardiovascular assessment.
investigations.rationale: CG109 supports clinician follow-up of blackout or syncope events, recurrent-event history, postural context, cardiovascular assessment, and already selected investigations without assigning a cause.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led orthostatic assessment, repeat or previous ECG review, ambulatory ECG selection, further investigation, and specialist cardiovascular assessment.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2 and 1.3 — postural hypotension and specialist cardiovascular assessment

investigations.text: Supports documented discussion of the event, tests, driving, safety, and what to do if another event occurs.
investigations.rationale: The information and recurrence section supports documenting clinician-provided information, safety discussion, questions, and arranged follow-up without generating driving or recurrence instructions.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documented discussion of the event, tests, driving, safety, and what to do if another event occurs.

source_limitations: Event recurrence and chronology, witness account, injuries, prodrome and recovery, posture, triggers, examination, ECG and monitoring, structural disease, diagnosis, driving, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
